narrative.intro: Through FY2026 to date federal contract obligations total $369B. Total award value figures are full contract values, not single-year obligations. The largest NAICS markets by award value include Commercial and Institutional Building Construction at $39.7B, Aircraft Manufacturing at $31.6B, Facilities Support Services at $30.3B, and Engineering Services at $30.1B. Other top markets are Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers at $27B, Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology) at $25.5B, and Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing at $23.1B. Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B), Ship Building and Repairing ($15.2B), and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B) round out the ten largest NAICS markets by award value in FY2026 to date. These ten NAICS categories represent the highest single NAICS award totals reported in the ranking for the period.

sections.heading: Which NAICS sectors show the largest federal award values?
sections.body: Commercial and Institutional Building Construction shows the largest award value at $39.7B. Aircraft Manufacturing ($31.6B), Facilities Support Services ($30.3B), and Engineering Services ($30.1B) follow closely. Several service and technology sectors also appear among the largest markets, including Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B) and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B).

sections.heading: How many distinct industry types appear among the top NAICS markets?
sections.body: The top NAICS ranking lists construction, manufacturing, services, R&D, and insurance industries among its largest markets. The ten largest categories include building construction, aircraft and guided missile manufacturing, facilities support, engineering, health insurance, R&D, computer services, and ship building. Award values for these categories range from $39.7B down to $13.1B. Together they define the highest-value NAICS markets in FY2026 to date.

faq.q: What are the biggest federal contracting industries?
faq.a: The biggest federal contracting industries are Commercial and Institutional Building Construction ($39.7B), Aircraft Manufacturing ($31.6B), Facilities Support Services ($30.3B), Engineering Services ($30.1B), and Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers ($27B). Other top industries by award value include Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology) ($25.5B), Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing ($23.1B), Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B), Ship Building and Repairing ($15.2B), and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B).
